name: Turruncún Ruins Circuit
name_original: Muro de Aguas, Valdeperillo, Turruncún – circular por Senda de los Cuñaos
description: This challenging 24.5-mile mountain bike route explores abandoned villages and historical sites, including the Ruins of Turruncún.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Muro De Aguas offer diverse natural landscapes and varied terrain in La Rioja, Spain. The region is characterized by mountainous areas, including the Sierra de Achena, providing elevations suitable for challenging rides. Riders can expect a mix of forests, rivers, and unique geological features like barrancos (ravines) and dinosaur footprint sites. This topography creates a varied environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Muro De Aguas The most popular mountain bike trail is Turruncún Ruins Circuit, a 24.5 miles (39.5 km) difficult trail that takes about 3 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route explores mountainous terrain and passes by historical ruins. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cornago, Valdeperillo – circular con vistas al Castillo de Cervera, a difficult 34.6 miles (55.7 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and views of a castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Senda de los Cuñados – Ermita de San Marcos loop from Arnedo, a 28.3 miles (45.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Muro De Aguas is defined by mountainous terrain, barrancos (ravines), and diverse scenery including forests and rivers. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Muro De Aguas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Muro De Aguas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Muro De Aguas?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Muro De Aguas, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Muro De Aguas?
A: Yes, Muro De Aguas offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 6 easy-rated mountain bike trails, providing a gentler introduction to the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Muro De Aguas?
A: The terrain around Muro De Aguas is predominantly mountainous, featuring diverse landscapes including forests, rivers, and unique geological formations like barrancos (ravines). You can expect varied surfaces and elevations, with options ranging from moderate to difficult.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Muro De Aguas?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Muro De Aguas are circular. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993654563/turruncun-ruins-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turruncún Ruins Circuit&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de los Cuñados – Ermita de San Marcos loop from Herce&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What are some notable natural features or attractions I might see along the trails?
A: Muro De Aguas is known for its unique geological features. You might encounter dinosaur footprints at sites like El Chorrón del Saltadero, Perosancio, and Valdete. The region also offers scenic viewpoints and a rich fauna, including various raptors like Griffon vultures. For specific points of interest, consider exploring highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles visible from the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of historical landmarks. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993872259/cornago-valdeperillo-circular-con-vistas-al-castillo-de-cervera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornago, Valdeperillo – circular con vistas al Castillo de Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides vistas of a castle. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt; as a nearby attraction.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Muro De Aguas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Muro De Aguas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Over 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and challenging rides.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by thermal baths or other relaxing spots?
A: Yes, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; is a great option. This route takes you past the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and its thermal baths&lt;/a&gt;, offering a chance to combine your ride with a relaxing stop.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Muro De Aguas?
A: Muro De Aguas experiences a Mediterranean climate with dry, hot summers and mild winters. While specific trail conditions vary, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense summer heat.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Muro De Aguas has 14 difficult-rated mountain bike trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993654563/turruncun-ruins-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turruncún Ruins Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a challenging 39.5 km route with significant elevation changes, exploring mountainous terrain and historical ruins.

Q: Can I find trails that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Muro De Aguas provides numerous opportunities for panoramic views. Many trails ascend to higher elevations, offering stunning vistas of the Sierra de Achena and the surrounding diverse landscapes. The region is known for its scenic viewpoints.
tips: A town almost abandoned and half in ruins.

The population of Turruncún reached about 300 inhabitants at the beginning of the 20th century. A large part of these Turruncunese were engaged in mining. The coal mines that are found throughout the entire area were the economic and social engine of Turruncún. However, when these mines were closed, Turruncún slowly declined.
For me one of the most beautiful paths on today's route. Not excessively technical, although it does have a delicate point.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain biking route is approximately 39.5 km long and typically takes around 3 hours and 49 minutes to complete. However, given its 'difficult' classification and technical sections, riders should factor in additional time for breaks, navigating delicate points, and potentially pushing their bikes.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as a 'difficult' mountain bike ride, demanding very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for technical sections, estimated at STS S3-S6 on the Single Track Scale, where they might need to dismount and push their bikes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Muro de Aguas, Valdeperillo, Turruncún mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the route is circular and starts near Muro de Aguas. Riders typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Muro de Aguas, or other nearby villages, to begin their journey.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse terrain, including dedicated trails (Senda), roads (Carretera and Carretera nacional), and narrow paths (Sendero estrecho). You'll encounter gypsum slopes and low mountain ranges, with the Senda de los Cuñados portion having some 'delicate points' that require advanced handling.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Muro de Aguas, Valdeperillo, Turruncún route?
faqs.answer: The route offers unique historical and natural scenery. Key highlights include passing through Valdeperillo, an almost abandoned village, and the poignant Ruins of Turruncún, a former mining town. The Senda de los Cuñados itself is considered one of the most scenic paths, offering views of Peña Isasa and the Arnedo depression.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs, given the trail's technical difficulty, length, and potential for remote sections, it's advisable to consider your dog's fitness and ability to handle challenging terrain. Always keep dogs on a leash, especially near historical sites, livestock, or in areas where wildlife may be present.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in La Rioja, making the long and demanding route more challenging. Winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail, especially since it's in a Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Reserva de la Biosfera Valles del Leza, Jubera, Cidacos y Alhama. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the reserve. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to protect the natural and historical environment.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of various other paths. You will ride along parts of official trails such as Sierras Riojanas, Subida a Isasa, Camino a Peña Isasa, Camino del Regadío, and Camino a Turruncún, among others, adding to the diverse experience.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring ample water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes/patch kit), a first-aid kit, and appropriate protective gear. A GPS device with the route downloaded is highly recommended due to the varied terrain and potential for remote sections. Sun protection and layers of clothing are also advisable.

faqs.question: What makes the abandoned village of Turruncún so significant on this route?
faqs.answer: The Ruins of Turruncún offer a unique and poignant glimpse into the region's past. Once a thriving mining town with hundreds of inhabitants, its decline and abandonment provide a 'ghost village' experience. Riders can explore the ruins of its church, former houses, a school, and underground wineries, making it a significant historical and visual highlight of the ride.
